items.headline: **vLLM**: Last report covered v0.18 / v0.19. Now confirming **v0.19.1** (Transfo
items.diff_narrative: **vLLM**: Last report covered v0.18 / v0.19. Now confirming **v0.19.1** (Transformers v5.5.4 upgrade, Gemma4 bug fix, via the Apr 3 rc0).

items.headline: **Claude Opus 4.7 broadens on Bedrock**: Last report covered the GA notice only.
items.diff_narrative: **Claude Opus 4.7 broadens on Bedrock**: Last report covered the GA notice only. Now we see **simultaneous deployment to 27 AWS regions** plus the new infrastructure engine's "zero operator access" design (customer prompts unreadable by Anthropic / AWS operators).

items.headline: **OpenClaw weekly quality cadence**: Last report emphasized NemoClaw. Adding tha
items.diff_narrative: **OpenClaw weekly quality cadence**: Last report emphasized NemoClaw. Adding that OpenClaw itself shipped **2026.4.12 / 2026.4.14**, defaulting Claude Opus 4.7, adding Gemini TTS, cloud LanceDB memory, and Feishu improvements.

items.headline: **AI Security**: Last report mentioned "growing indirect prompt injection." Now
items.diff_narrative: **AI Security**: Last report mentioned "growing indirect prompt injection." Now we have a concrete **PoC over GitHub Actions across Anthropic / Google / Microsoft** plus **Fortinet FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-35616** under real-world attack.

items.headline: **Local LLM implementation matures**: Last report focused on Bonsai's announceme
items.diff_narrative: **Local LLM implementation matures**: Last report focused on Bonsai's announcement and benchmarks. This window pivots to **CUDA deploy + GGUF operations hands-on writeups (Apr 17, 19)** and the `bankai` XOR-patch post-hoc adaptation method.

items.headline: angles
items.diff_narrative: **New angles**: Simon Willison's Apr 19 "Headless everything for personal AI," MIT Technology Review's Apr 21 "10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now," Cadence × NVIDIA's CadenceLIVE SV 2026 partnership, and Anthropic's annual revenue passing $30B (closing on OpenAI) are all new in this window.

items.headline: **Qwen visual evaluation**: Last report described "examples beating Opus 4.7 on
items.diff_narrative: **Qwen visual evaluation**: Last report described "examples beating Opus 4.7 on visual reasoning" abstractly. Now we have **Simon Willison's pelican-drawing test, where 35B-A3B running locally on a laptop clearly beats Opus 4.7** — a concrete reproducible experiment.
